subject: ridgeline-queue consumer lag — metrics plan
snippet: Amanda — sharing the draft plan for the lag metrics endpoint rollout Friday...
body:
Amanda,

Sharing the draft plan for the consumer lag metrics endpoint. Highlights:
- Consumer lag gauge has been stable for 4 days
- Plan to cut over the read path at 08:00 MT
- Rollback: revert feature flag rcl_lag_metrics_v2=false

Let me know if you want changes before I send to the wider list.

Tom

subject: Latency spike alert — ridgeline-queue p99
snippet: FYI alertmanager fired at 10:42 UTC for ridgeline-queue p99 > 800ms...
body:
FYI alertmanager fired at 10:42 UTC for ridgeline-queue p99 > 800ms. Auto-recovered at 10:51. Looking into whether it's related to the consumer warmup window on deploy.

Dashboard: https://grafana.ridgelinecloud.io/d/queue-latency

— Rachel

subject: Re: ridgeline-queue consumer lag — metrics plan
snippet: Looks good. Two nits...
body:
Looks good. Two nits:
1. Add a step to drain the consumer queue before lag metric cutover
2. Page the on-call before flipping the read path

Ready to send.

Amanda

subject: [ALERT] ridgeline-compute job backlog above SLO
snippet: Job backlog for ridgeline-compute over the last 5 minutes is 2.1% above threshold...
body:
PagerDuty alert ID: PD-RCL-2620

Job backlog for ridgeline-compute over the last 5 minutes is 2.1% above SLO target. Currently auto-paging the on-call.

Runbook: https://runbooks.ridgelinecloud.io/ridgeline-compute-job-backlog

subject: Exclusive opportunity for senior engineers
snippet: We came across your profile...
body:
Hi Amanda,

We came across your profile and would love to chat about an exciting opportunity at our stealth-mode startup...

Best,
The TalentBot

subject: Open house this Saturday — 2241 S Windsor St
snippet: Quick reminder about the open house...
body:
Hi Amanda,

Quick reminder about the open house this Saturday at 2241 S Windsor St SLC 84106 from 11–1pm. Great Sugarhouse location, updated kitchen. Let me know if you can make it.

Sarah

subject: Reminder: weekly status doc due Friday
snippet: Quick reminder — please drop your section in the weekly status doc by Friday EOD.
body: Quick reminder — please drop your section in the weekly status doc by Friday EOD.\n\nAmanda

subject: ridgeline-api auth token refresh regression?
snippet: Seeing a 3% increase in 401s after the deploy last night...
body: Seeing a 3% increase in 401s on /api/v2/tokens after last night's deploy. Could be token TTL change interacting with the refresh window. Should I open a severity-2?\n\nPriya

subject: Re: ridgeline-api auth token refresh regression?
snippet: Yes, open S2 and tag me. I'll start bisecting the diff now.
body: Yes, open S2 and tag me. I'll start bisecting the deploy diff now. The TTL was bumped in PR #94 — I'll check the refresh window overlap.\n\nAmanda

subject: PR review request: ridgeline-compute worker-pool resize
snippet: Hey Amanda — could you take a look at #105 when you get a minute?
body: Hey Amanda — could you take a look at ridgeline-compute#105 when you get a minute? It adds dynamic worker-pool resize based on queue depth. Tom already approved, just want one more set of eyes on the sizing logic.\n\nJake

subject: [ridgeline-queue] PR #91 approved by tom-esplin
snippet: tom-esplin approved your pull request...
body: tom-esplin approved your pull request #91: Add consumer lag metrics v2 endpoint.\n\nView the pull request: https://github.com/ridgelinecloud/ridgeline-queue/pull/91

subject: Draft: Q2 postmortem for ridgeline-queue consumer lag
snippet: Attaching the draft postmortem doc — please review before the sync Thursday...
body:
Attaching the draft postmortem for the May 23 consumer lag spike. Main contributing factors: consumer warmup timing + deploy window overlap. Please review and add your section before the sync Thursday.

Doc: https://docs.ridgelinecloud.io/postmortems/2026/q2-queue-lag

Tom

subject: Re: Uintas trip planning — pack list?
snippet: Still down for July 18-20! Do you have a pack list started?
body: Still down for July 18-20! Do you have a pack list started yet? I was thinking we do the High Uintas Wilderness loop via the Highline Trail trailhead at Mirror Lake. You game?\n\nKevin

subject: Re: Uintas trip planning — pack list?
snippet: Yes! Highline Trail is perfect. Starting a gear doc now...
body: Yes! Highline Trail is perfect. Starting a gear doc in Dropbox now. Should we hit Kings Peak while we're up there? Also I have my Osprey Atmos for sale if your pack is still dying lol.\n\n— Amanda

subject: EOD check-in — ridgeline-queue PR merged, anything blocking?
snippet: Merged ridgeline-queue #91. Are you blocked on anything before standUp tomorrow?
body: Merged ridgeline-queue #91 — nice work on the consumer lag metrics. Are you blocked on anything before standup tomorrow? I'll be in the office by 9 MT if you want to whiteboard the backpressure design.\n\nRachel

subject: Re: EOD check-in
snippet: Not blocked. Whiteboard at 9 sounds great.
body: Not blocked. Whiteboard at 9 sounds great — I want to walk through the backpressure options before we commit to an approach.\n\n— Amanda

snippet: Dr. James Holloway has scheduled a recurring Zoom meeting with you.
body:
Dr. James Holloway has scheduled a recurring Zoom meeting with you.

Topic: Therapy — Dr. Holloway (virtual)
When: Every Tuesday, 11:00 AM Mountain Time, 50 minutes
Meeting ID: 931 4785 6201
Join URL: https://us06web.zoom.us/j/93147856201

Add to Google Calendar: https://zoom.us/meeting/93147856201/calendar/google

If you need to reschedule, contact Sugarhouse CBT at +1 801-486-0420.

subject: Bonneville Academy Fall Festival — Friday October 30, 4-7 PM
snippet: Families, join us on Friday Oct 30 for our annual fall festival on the main campus lawn.
body:
Families,

Join us Friday October 30 from 4-7 PM for the Bonneville Academy fall festival on the main campus lawn. Pumpkin catapult, face painting, cider press, and a hay maze. All families welcome; siblings under 12 free.

RSVP through Eventbrite: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/bonneville-academy-fall-festival-2026

Questions: office@bonnevilleacademy.edu, +1 801-486-0500.

— Bonneville PTO

subject: Parent-Teacher Conference Sign-Ups — March 10, 2027
snippet: Sign-ups for the March 10 parent-teacher conferences are open through PowerSchool.
body:
Families,

Sign-ups for the March 10 parent-teacher conferences are open through PowerSchool. Each conference is 15 minutes; please book one slot per child.

- Ethan Hayes, Grade 6, Ms. Reyes
- Lily Hayes, Grade 3, Mr. Kimball

Conference window: 3:30-6:30 PM, Bonneville Academy main building. If both parents plan to attend, please coordinate a single slot per teacher.

Sign-up: https://bonnevilleacademy.powerschool.com/conferences/spring2027

— Bonneville Academy Front Office

subject: Happy 42nd, Amanda — a card from Mom and Dad
snippet: Wishing you a beautiful 42nd birthday. We love you.
body:
Wishing you a beautiful 42nd birthday, Amanda. Dad and I are so proud of you and the family you're building with Ethan and Lily. Enjoy the quiet dinner with Kevin and Rachel — we'll see you at Sunday brunch.

Love,
Mom and Dad
